Hi Everyone -
I thought I would contribute to the collective knowledge here with my journey busting using Rivea Corymbosa Seeds, AKA "RC Seeds", AKA Ololiuqui. I will keep a log of it all in the format I've shown below. First I will give some quick background. (To skip to just the busting log, scroll to the bottom).
I'm a 35 year old male, first started getting CH when I was sixteen, in 2001. Cycles are generally ~7 weeks, and have been getting further and further apart the older I get, with ~3 years between cycles. When I was a teen I did the pharma route, finding mixed results with Prednisone taper and Inderal. But once those stopped working, a neurologist with a bad attitude started trying other drugs on me, and I started reading about the experiences of other CHers on clusterheadaches.com and here on clusterbusters, I said "no thank you" to the pharma route.
2003 - 2010
Sloppily experimenting with busting, different vitamins, prednisone/Inderal etc. I was 18-25 during this time, and you know how people are at that age! I had mixed results with mushrooms, and also had very mixed results with the high! One of the most enjoyable nights in my entire life was on mushrooms, and it stopped the CH dead in its tracks. I also had some of the most intensely anxious times in my life, and it didn't stop the CH. I attempted RC Seeds during this time, but back then the recommended starting dose was very low. I think I started with 8 seeds! So, I found no impact from using them.
2010 - 2020
Started the Vitamin D3 regimen. The D3 brought the severity of the pain during attacks WAY down. I've never been able to fully control a cycle using just the D3 regimen, but it has helped tremendously. During this time I also started using oxygen - *properly*! I was prescribed oxygen as a teenager, but was using the wrong mask and didn't have enough air flow, so I concluded that it didn't work for me. This was a total game-changer, obviously. I also started realizing that when I eat, I get attacks. I started talking to Batch a lot during this time. We talked about the body's PH and how keeping the body more alkaline could help. So I started drinking more lemon water, making tweaks like that, that I think helped a little bit. I tried 5-MEO-DALT for two cycles during this time as well. The results were mixed and I did not like the idea of weighing out these tiny, tiny increments of a random chemical I purchased in a sketchy way on the internet and ingesting it.
Latest Cycle - Started Nov. 2020
This cycle has been different for a number of reasons: 1) It came faster than expected. Typically the pain free time between my cycles increases by 3 months each cycle. It is crazy how accurately it has followed it my entire life! 3 months between my first cycle and my second, then six months, then 9 months, etc. etc. I was all the way up to three years between cycles, and then this latest one came after 2.5 years. Hi everyone! I want to share my successful bust story and share what one should expect moving to or coming to Portugal with a CH.
A breif note on my case:
I have episodic CH
Last year, my doctor in Turkey prescribed me Cymbalta (Duloxetine, an SNRI) as an antidepressant. I am still taking 60 mg daily
I am a 6’6”, 200-pound male (which may or may not be relevant to the RC dose and side effects)
I moved to Portugal in early February, and my cycle started in mid-February
I hadn't had any busting experience before
CH treatment in Portugal:
You must have a prescription from a local doctor to get triptans. I assume a prescription from any doctor in Europe would be accepted, but mine was in Turkish and wasn’t accepted
In mid-February, the earliest available appointment with a neurologist in Porto or Braga was in mid-March. I went to a family doctor who gave me a prescription, but he could only prescribe a small number of pills, like high single digit. To get a sufficient supply for a full cycle, you need to see a neurologist
I was lucky enough to find an appointment in late February in a small town in northern Portugal. The doctor was quite knowledgeable and prescribed me an entire warchest of triptans. I think I now have enough for 2–3 bad cycles
Oxygen tanks are not easy to obtain here. You must be registered in the Portuguese healthcare system, and only a pulmonologist can prescribe them to you. The neurologist tried to get me prescription without me going to a pulmonologist, but ultimately failed to do so.
By the time I visited the neurologist, I had already ordered RC seeds. So, I decided to postpone jumping through all those hoops until later to prepare for the next cycle
My busting story:
I ordered RC seeds from
http://dutch-headshop.be
.
Thank you
@Shaun brearley
for sharing this website in another thread. I am sorry if it was originally posted by anyone else
These guys offer various seeds and mushrooms, but you can’t order the latter to Portugal since mushrooms are illegal here. You can still order the seeds, spores, and growing kits, though.
Keep in mind that the website only accepts bank transfers, which means you need to allow 2–3 days for the payment to be processed. I received my seeds in 8 days—3 days for payment processing and 5 days for delivery.
The first treatment:
I waited 5 days after taking my last triptan. I was lucky enough to have relatively mild attacks (as far as CH goes), so I managed without triptans and oxygen
I followed
@MoxieGirl
's recipe you can find here
https://clusterbusters.org/forums/topic/2353-moxiegirls-seed-recipe/
On Monday, I took one package of RC seeds, which contained 40 seeds. After doing the floating test, I found only 3 bad seeds. I then ground, soaked, and drank the concoction made from the remaining 37 seeds.
I didn't have a trip or any side effects whatsoever.
On Tuesday, I had a slapback. It wasn't a full-blown attack, so it was manageable without taking a painkiller or oxygen, however, it was sharp

Towards the end of 2024 I was finding reduced efficacy of MM and RC seeds in a manner many here will be familiar with. Because of that I tried Ketamine for the first time and the results were promising. I have posted on various other topics during my experiments but thought I'd present a summary here of my experiences one year later.
Couple of caveats first - I am not a trained medical professional so none of this is medical advice, but through neccessity I have become quite knowledgable about medications and biology, I'm sure you know what I mean. I have based my dosages and methods on available research papers for Ketamine treatment of migraine. Unfortunately where I am Ketamine is very tightly controlled so I am sourcing it off the record, however I am confident in its purity - unlike many other meds Ketamine is presented as clear crystaline shards rather than any sort of powder, it's not something that is normally cut with bulking agents etc and it's so cheap that it doesn't require cutting.
A word on safety - please thoroughly research K if you plan on trying it, it is a dissociative anaesthetic and those words alone should hint at the potential dangers involved. Remember to also check for contra-indications although it appears to be pretty resiliant on that front. Also worht mentioning that long-term use can cause permanent inflammatory damage to the bowel and/or bladder. This aspect of K is poorly understood so always err on the side of taking less where possible. Please do your own research into this. Addiction potential is low but it can be compulsive so worth checking your behaviour on a regular basis.
My 2024 experiences with Ketamine coincided with me also trying other things, so I decided that 2025 would be Ketamine-only to see how successful it was. And one year later I'm pleased to report that I've had my clearest year in probably 15 years. Hopefully my suggested regime will help some others find similar success.
Many Ketamine migraine studies involve nasal sprays with 15mg doses taken on onset, repeated after 15 minutes if necessary. That was my starting point - 15mg insufflated ketamine at the onset of a cluster attack, repeated 2 or 3 more times as necessary during an attack . Most of the time this completely aborted it, and even during what I believe was probably a KIP 10 the numbing effect of 3 doses helped reduce the sensations almost entirely (yet the experience still felt like a KIP 10 without most of the pain,very hard to describe how it felt that way). This must be done in a safe environment (bedroom is perfect) as you will definitely need to lie down for more than one dose.
Aside from dosing during attacks, I also took a bedtime single dose of 20mg if I was developing shadows that evening. By the beginning of Feb 2025 I was no longer suffering attacks and simply taking a bedtime dose when shadows appeared.
That's pretty much it. One year on and if memory serves correctly I've not had a single attack since. I treat the fi

Hello, Cluster Busters!  I am the one they call Onglamesh, and I've been a skull crushing, writhing crybaby for 10 years.  This is my first time posting publicly about my most effective form of relief... I fear possible repercussions for coming out and saying what I'm going to say, but my ever increasing contemplation of the term "suicide headache" has convinced me that I
MUST
share this information... what if somebody on the brink of ending it all happens upon this and finds new hope?  I've decided that possibility is FAR more significant to me than the negative... if I get busted for busting, due to talking about how I do it, then so be it... at least I can feel like I may have helped somebody abandon the thought of taking their own life.  Because I can honestly say that I've thought about it more than enough times, and I may have done it if I hadn't stumbled upon such exquisite relief.
I learned about cluster headache only 2 years into my struggle.  I also learned immediately that all the medications offered by doctors are very pitifully ineffective for most of us, and that the majority of sufferers seek medical help but continue to suffer because the medical world gives less than a shit about us.  I learned that most doctors don't even know what CH is, and will misdiagnose you or refer you to a dozen specialists who also don't know what it is and send you on the scavenger hunt for ineffective drugs with really bad side effects.  So I must confess, I've never been to see a doctor about my condition, I gave up on that idea before trying... and for that, I thank all you brave souls who have been running the medical gauntlet and sharing your experiences with newcomers like me.
I've taken acetaminophen, ibuprofen, and naproxen as pathetic excuses for relief over the years.  I would take whichever one was handy, or combine the acetaminophen with one of the NSAIDs, knowing that it will barely do anything if anything at all... but rationalizing that it's better than doing nothing to try and stop the pain.  I've been given darvocet, morphine, dilaudid, hydrocodone, and probably various other pain meds when somebody has one to spare.  None of them took my pain away; if I'm lucky, they'd get me a little bit high so that I care a little bit less.  So I gave up on prescription drugs 7 years ago and just took the OTCs with little to no benefit.  I went through a period of alcoholism (now sober for 5 years) not caused by the headache, but certainly utilized at times to help me dissociate from it.  Sure, alcohol triggers the headache, but if you drink yourself into oblivion, you won't even remember that you had one.  The problem with that method is you still wake up feeling like the hot pokers are rigged up to a jackhammer.
I lived in constant fear of the Beast, knowing that Murphy's Law indicates it will strike again while I'm at work or on a date, or trying to get a good night's sleep.  The Beast has a name, and it is Murphy.  If I didn't have a wonderful

Couldn't be more relieved to be writing this post, but I'm delighted to report that I'm 9 days pain free, so I am feeling quite confident that the beast is back in the box for the foreseeable. I wasn't able to find a huge number of detailed bust reports for LSD, so I wanted to write up a report that was quite thorough, in case it's useful to someone (even if it's just future me!)
Context: I'm 27, male, based in London. This is my third cycle so far, so I'm still very new to ECH. My first two cycles were two years apart. In my first cycle, I received a diagnosis after 6 weeks, found Custerbusters after 8 weeks, and spent the following 2 weeks on a slightly manic LSD binge, after being quite desperate for some relief. The cycle ended after that, unsurprisingly, though I was never sure if it was the L or the cycle ending naturally.
After two years pain free, it returned in 2017. In that cycle, I only had access to MM. It took 5 weeks to break it, but got there in the end. I then had 2 and a half years pain free before my current cycle, which followed a missed maintenance dose and an errant glass of wine in June.
Day 1: My cycle with a relatively manageable K5. I went for a relatively modest ~40ug LSD, mostly sub-hallucinogenic, in accordance with the regimen as it stood in 2015. I experienced mild slapbacks (a pressure building in my forehead) as the effects wore off. In the days that followed, my attacks remained consistent in terms of pain, with frequency staying at/below 1 per day.
Day 5: I got hit with a K8, accompanied by nausea and vomiting. I dosed again afterwards, around ~50ug. When I came down, I was hit with a K8 slapback, and then a K9 the following day. Frequency was now up to around 2-3/day, often with one attack significantly worse than others.
Day 9: Dosed again, this time ~100ug, after coming across more up to date advice which suggested 'recreational doses' for busting with L. I decided to bring the bust forward a day, basically because I had to be functional the next day, but also because in my recreational use I'd found that my tolerance level was usually unnoticeable after 3 days off. Perhaps this was a bad call though, as I got hit with 2 and a half hours of K10, hands down the worst attack of my life. Decided after this that I needed to get hold of some O2, as the caffeine and hot showers weren't particularly helpful anymore.
Day 12: Finally was able to start the D3 regimen. Also started cutting chocolate from my diet and stopped skipping meals, as I began to suspect these were triggers.
Day 16: After several days of battling my doctor for it, I finally got my hands on some O2. Can't believe I went without it for the past 16 days, and the two cycles before this one! I felt giddy with relief after killing my first attack with it, made a mental note to ensure I had it from day 1 of my next cycle.
Day 17: Dosed again with around~100ug LSD, very slapback heavy trip but super manageable with my tank by my side.

I thought I'd take a moment and pen my whole
Cluster Headache
story (as briefly as I can). I find I post all, or part of it, in a number of new member posts quite frequently when people ask if
Busting
really works, and if there is any hope out there to be had, so thought I'd post it here, then I can just drop a link to it in posts. Lazy, I know.
Migraines
run in my family. I've had them all my life, all my siblings have them, my nieces and nephews and my 2 children. They have just always been a part of our family. My triggers are normally: stress, being over tired, hunger, thirst, changes in weather, sudden cold, strong smells, bright lights, and probably a few other things. I would normally go a year or so with none, or very few migraines, then be in a stressful situation and have a bunch.
In 2006/2007 I transitioned from male to female. In 2006 I started on a high dose of Oestrogen and an Anti-androgen designed to shut off my Testosterone. On 31 Dec 2006, at around 10pm, I got a headache. I wasn't ringing in the new year with friends that year. As chance would have it, I was home alone and not drinking. Just having a quiet night to myself. So the headache wasn't a result of alcohol or loud music or anything. Just a headache.
That headache lasted for
7 months,
and finally started breaking up in July when I'd got a few days here and there pain free. Then it got even better in August after I flew to Thailand for surgery. This was diagnosed as
Chronic Daily Headaches
(although at the time, it was in fact one non-stop headache).
That very first week of 2007 was shocking! On top of this headache, I got a bad
Migraine
and my first ever
Cluster Headache
. At that time in my life, my migraines were lasting 2-7 days, 3-4 days being the average. The
Cluster Headache
totally blew me away. I had no idea what it was.
I started out getting 2-5 clusters a week. I would have one Tuesday night at either 7pm or 7:30pm, another one or two on Thursdays, the occasional Wednesday and one or two over the weekend. That was pretty regular my first year. Plus, of course, nearly weekly migraines that were lasting days.
2007 was my Year of Hell. It was, quite literally, just variations in the level of pain for 7 months.
Thankfully, my GP had a good friend who had clusters, so she correctly diagnosed them on my first visit and got me straight to a neurologist. The neurologist said he had come across a number of transsexuals who got clusters. He believed that my body was predisposed to having them, and the change in my hormones just triggered it. Once triggered, there was no going back, unfortunately. It's pretty accepted that clusters are linked to the Hypothalamus, and also pretty accepted that changing one's hormones alters the Hypothalamus.

DMT is a very powerful endogenous hallucinogen. I don't recommend it as a first, or even second line of therapy for cluster headache. For those with a stubborn, chronic condition, this may be helpful.
vaporized DMT has some advantages over MM or L
-tolerance fades within an hour. This means that you can redose pretty much whenever, without fear of "shutting the door".
-The onset of effects is within seconds, making it a good abortive and takes at most 10-20 minutes to get back to baseline. The duration of treatment is more or less up to the user.
- You can dissolve DMT freebase in e-liquid and use it in a commercial off the shelf vape mod, typically used by people who vape nicotine. This provides a portable and socially inconspicuous way to abort a headache in an emergency without interfering with your busting success.
So first...
The DMT:
DMT may be extracted with relative ease from plant matter known to contain it. This includes Mimosa Hostilis root bark (MHRB) or Acacia Confusa root bark (ACRB). Here is a link to a good protocol.
https://wiki.dmt-nexus.me/Marsofold's_tek
As a side note, I would skip the washing step at the end. You risk losing a lot of product. Just decant as much of the recrystallization solvent as you can and let your product dry in front of a fan in a well ventilated space. Once the it appears totally dry, it isn't. You need to wait about a week until the smell of naptha is totally gone before proceeding to the next step.
The e-liquid:
You need to purchase USP food grade propylene glycol (PG) and USP food grade vegetable glycerin (VG). They are both available online from amazon.com. I like to use a ratio of 30% PG to 70% VG. If you are allergic to PG, use an all VG solution with a few drops of distilled water to lower the viscosity of the solution to allow proper wicking into the cotton of your atomizer. An effective concentration of DMT/mL is 100-300mg/mL, depending on personal preference. This will be high enough to achieve an effect, but low enough to prevent accidental transdimensional transport. When you add the DMT freebase to your e-liquid solution, it won't dissolve until you heat it. Just place your eliquid container under the hot water at the faucet. With some gentle shaking, it will go into solution in a few minutes.
The Vape -
Now that you have your liquid, pick out a good sub-ohm direct to lung (DTL) vape mod. The setup is often sold in pieces including the mod, the battery, and the tank. Stick to a tank that supports an atomizer around .3 ohm. This will give you vapor that is easy on the lungs, but hot enough to carry your product. I like this one because it is a kit that comes with everything you need, except extra atomizers
https://www.myvaporstore.com/Eleaf-iStick-Power-Nano-Kit-p/elis-ispwn10.htm
(Eleaf iStick Power Nano Kit) and is very small. 25 Watts of power seems ideal. Although many mods support up to 80W power.
Alternatively - You may vaporize DMT with the "sherlock style glass vapor genie". A bowl

Been doing some experimentation with the seeds recently and thought I’d share my results:) Have not had the opportunity to do any lab testing for actual LSA content or anything, but I’m getting the impression that color has more to do with general quality than anything. Although, floating definitely seems to help pick out most of the darker older seeds. Considering that most folks pay a pretty penny for these guys, I figure every seed really does count! I have so many old ones to experiment with and need to step up the quality of what we have been receiving lately, so here goes:)
When I float test the seeds, I find that many of the darker colored ones do float, especially the really old gray ones. (unfortunately the best picture of that did not turn out well. (Will have to take another one next batch)
But in the second picture you can tell that even some darker ones sunk to the bottom. I took one light brown seed that was floating and that darker one that I circled that was sinking and crushed them both. It was clear that the darker one really had hardly any meat left on the inside.
I then took a gradient of different colored dry seeds and crushed them and found that the darker they got the less meat they had in them, thus the older they were. Any that were grayish poofed to dust almost instantly. The lighter the color, the fresher the meat seemed to be.
So it seems that it is well worth keeping the lighter floaters or at least individually crushing them to see if they have any meat in them that would be worth using in your brew.
I noticed that some seeds had little holes at the top where they used to be connected to the plant. I crush tested these in different colors and largely found the same results. Though they were more likely to have older looking meat in them than the ones that didn’t have holes when lighter in color.
Also, when I was lazy and crushed all of the seeds, regardless of color, the resulting brew was about as dark as coffee and tasted even worse if you can imagine that. (mind you, the seeds that I am experimenting with are hopefully a lot older than the ones you might be buying) I will post comparison pictures later when I make my next brew.
Oddly enough, I still have not tried making the brew with peppermint tea as some people have suggested. I have tried making it with a little bit of vodka and lemon juice and water or just lemon juice and water, or just water though. I would love to hear anybody’s experiences on the differences as I think I take them too often right now to be a real good judge here. I definitely notice a difference in only using water as opposed to lemon juice and water though. So I would say go the lemon tek route at least!
Still not sure how long is best to let them soak, but I would imagine that if you’re not drinking the sludge, or adding lemon juice or anything like that, you would want to let them soak as long as possible. I have gotten desperate before and strained off a little almost immediately afte

My personal belief.  I was getting 4 to 5 night time attacks.  Slapbacks were pretty bad. Detox was hell.  But I got through it.

I sat on this post for 24 hours thinking it's too much and it's way too long. But there's something inside me still saying to share it for whoever it might touch, so here it goes...
Hello Everyone,
(*This turned into a book as I was writing it as I really allowed my passion to flow. Thank you to anyone willing to take the time to read it!*)
I am the mother of a 34 year old son who was diagnosed with CH when he was about 20. He wasn’t living with me at the time and when he spoke to me about his headaches he called them migraines so I only became aware of the actual diagnosis of Cluster Headaches and what that truly means recently. Since then I have been researching everything I can find on CH in my attempt to help him. This site has been invaluable for all of the information it contains as well as for everyone’s heartfelt sharing of their own experiences with CH. I am so appreciative!
While reading through the forums the other evening I had a major AHA moment as I started connecting the dots with CH and my son’s past medical history. As children he and his 2 years older sister constantly had strep throat and ear infections and were continuously on amoxicillin. Their younger brother somehow managed to avoid getting sick in a similar fashion. My husband died when my children were 4, 7 and 9 years old and losing the father they adored obviously impacted each of my children deeply, as it did me as well. 3 months after his death both of my 2 older kids underwent tonsillectomies since the strep infections were constant and amoxicillin was no longer keeping the infections at bay.
My daughter went on to become much healthier after the surgery, my son did not. While he stopped getting the strep throats that had plagued him for so long, he started getting constant stomach aches. Within a year he was sick so much of the time that he had missed over 1/3 of the school year. Our family practice doctors were at a loss to explain what was going on or diagnose him so referred him to a pediatric gastroenterologist who eventually diagnosed him with Crohn’s disease, a form of IBD (Inflammatory Bowel Disease) after tissue biopsy showed severe inflammation of parts of his bowel. The problem was that nothing Western Medicine had to offer him helped or alleviated his pain. Even prednisone didn’t seem to make much difference, all while he blew up like a balloon and suffered all the side effects that go along with steroids.
At this time I met a woman who happened to work for a Naturopath and she suggested that I take my son to see her. I had been working as a registered nurse for years so I was deeply steeped in allopathic medicine and hesitant to try something that wasn’t proven or necessarily approved by the medical community. Fortunately our family practice doctors were wonderful and encouraged me to take my son to see her. I've been wanting to post on this for a while now...This is probably going to be a long complicated post, most likely MORE than one post...but here goes
Harmala Alkaloids are substances found in varying degrees in plants like Syrian Rue, Banisteriopsis caapi vine, Passionflower and Tobacco (I'm sure there are more)Â  The two with the highest known amounts are the Syrian Rue seeds and the Banisteriopsis Caapi vines.
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harmala_alkaloid
The major alkaloids involved are Harmine, Harmaline, and Tetrahydroharmine.Â  These are all Beta Carbolines, They all inhibit the MAO A enzyme, and they all are considered RIMA's--Reversible Inhibitors of MAO subtype A.Â (For more info on RIMA's you can look into the pharmaceutical anti-depressant moclobomide, which has MAO A enzyme as it's mechanism of action.)Â  Â All 3 substances are completely legal in the U.S., as are both the Caapi Vine and Rue seeds.Â  I know I have read that they are hitting some of the same receptors as Psilocybin and LSA, but right now I can't find my sources.
But first we'll touch on MAO inhibitors in general.Â  Everything you hear about them sounds scary, everything seems to say, "Do Not Mix With MAO inhibitors"Â  We need to know that the substances contained in Caapi vines and Syrian Rue seeds are very different drugs than the old style pharmaceutical MAO inhibitors that were usually used for depression.Â  These older MAO inhibitors would inhibit TWO enzymes, MAO A and MAO B, where as the RIMA's only inhibit ONE enzyme, MAO A.Â  This is important because that means you still have an active MAO B enzyme, which makes your body perfectly able to still metabolize Tyramine.Â  With the older MAO inhibitors that inhibited BOTH enzymes you are not able to metabolize tyramine, this can lead to hypertensive crisis, and this is the biggest warning you will hear about with MAO inhibiting drugs.Â  Just remember--THIS IS NOT AN ISSUE WITH THE NATURAL MAO A INHIBITING DRUGS LIKE RUE OR CAAPI VINES.Â  The biggest thing that made me more comfortable with these drugs was when I realized that I have been taking them all my life without knowing it.Â  Certain foods, like chocolate, have this effect naturally andÂ  you'll never notice it.Â  Â Â In fact, Terence Mckenna claims that ALL tryptamines are RIMA's--this means the mushrooms, LSD, and LSA containing seeds that we are taking are all having this effect on us.Â  I can confirm this effect from the experience of smoking DMT while on mushrooms.Â  (More on that later)
So no risk of "hypertensive crisis"Â  Just forget about it.Â  There is the theory that if you are on RIMA's and eat lots and lots of tyramine containing foods that eventually you could "overwhelm" the system and cause this crisis, but I have NEVER heard of this actually happening.Â  From what I've read it seems more like people have been scared of MAO inhibitors for a long time and are reluctant to let go of that fear.Â  One of the biggest sources of tyramine is chees

LSA Extraction from HBWR using non-polar / polar method
Hawaiian Baby Woodrose ( Argyreia Nervosa ) seeds have considerably more LSA content than their cousin Morning Glory (
Rivea Corymbosa
). A typical RC dose might be 50 - 100 RC seeds, where the typical HBWR dose might be 2 - 4 seeds.
There are two problems associated with using HBWR seeds for busting.
1. Dose control - the amount of LSA varies considerably seed to seed and vendor to vendor. The weight per seed can vary between 50 - 130mg. Two 50mg seeds and you might feel nothing. Two 130mg seeds might be a very strong experience. This is very important for those of  us wishing to avoid side effects.
2. Nausea - though this can be curtailed by filing off the husk (from what I understand) this takes considerable effort.
Both of these issues are minimized be utilizing a 2 part extraction of the LSA from the seeds. The amount of LSA consumed can be controlled by preparing a larger group of seeds, then each dose is measure from this batch. Grind up 20 seeds, then weigh the material. Divide by 20 for an average weight for 1 seed.
The process is very easy and straightforward.
Non-polar solvent 50 hour soak of 20 seeds that have been ground or crushed. Once filtered and dried, this will yield between 1.7 and 2 grams of dry material ready for polar extraction that can be sealed and stored in a cool dry dark location (fridge). The material is weighed and divided by 20 to determine the per seed weight. This can be measured and used as an individual dose in the polar solvent 12 hour soak or, all of it can be used to create a 20ml ready to use tincture that can be stored in the fridge and dispensed by the drop - 20 drops per seed.
The attached HBWR LSA Extraction giude was written by a fellow clusterhead in Australia. I have followed his process with very good results.
Non-polar solvents are a petroleum product that evaporates quickly and cleanly. It leaves no residue of chemicals that will remain in your extraction. I have tested Ronsonol fluid and VM&P Naphthalene. Do not use Zippo or Coleman fuel. Both of these leave a chemical residue. They are also highly flammable and should be used in a well ventilated area.
The polar solvent is water. Purified, bottled or boil some water and allow it to cool to remove any chemicals (chlorine).
Dosing

What is 5-MeO-DALT, and why should you care?
The short version is that 5-MeO-DALT is a fairly new option for cluster headache patients who are comfortable self-administering treatments. It is a simple tryptamine that acts on serotonin receptors in the brain. When used carefully, 5-MeO-DALT is extremely safe and is proving to be very effective for some cluster headache patients.
Now, the FAQs:
Q: Who invented this cluster headache treatment?
A: Nobody invented it as a cluster headache treatment at all. The Â“recipeÂ” was devised by Alexander Shulgin in 2004, and later synthesized by a chemist. It became a relatively obscure Â“research chemicalÂ” used rarely as a recreational drug. More on that later. I stumbled upon 5-MeO-DALT because I was looking for a molecule that had some of the good properties of the things we know, like psilocybin and LSA (stopping my cluster headaches) without the bad properties (psychedelic trips, nausea, jail time).
Q: How does it work?
A: 5-MeO-DALT is a serotonergic. It works by engaging our serotonin receptors and sending a signal to our brains. The whole mechanism of exactly how this stops cluster headaches is still a mystery, but there are two key components: affinity and signaling. Affinity is how well a molecule Â“sticksÂ” to the right receptors, and signaling is the actual message that it sends to our brains. 5-MeO-DALT has a higher affinity than psilocybin for the 5HT2A receptor, and it seems to send the right signal.
Q: What makes it better?
A: IÂ’m not willing to say that itÂ’s Â“betterÂ” than other alternative treatments, but I will point out the advantages. One advantage is dosage control. When you take 15mg of 5-MeO-DALT, you are getting 15mg of 5-MeO-DALT. By comparison, when you take an extraction of rivea corymbosa seeds, you are getting an unknown amount of LSA along with hundreds of other chemicals, some of them volatile. When you take 1g of mushrooms, you are getting an unknown amount of psilocybin along with hundreds of other chemicals, some of them volatile. Why is this important? Imagine listening to your favorite song. Now imagine listening to your favorite song while vacuuming, the dog is barking, a baby is crying, and someone is ringing your doorbell. Same song? Not really. Every chemical you put in your body has a ripple effect. 5-MeO-DALT is a single molecule that does one thing.
Secondly, the side effects are almost non-existent. Many of us avoid using tryptamines over fears of a Â“tripÂ”. With a normal dose of 15mg of 5-MeO-DALT, most people notice absolutely no side effects at all. It has an extremely short half-life, so even if the user feels some effects (cold and sleepy are the two most common), it is out of your system in two hours.
Finally, it is very user-friendly. Accurate dosing of 5-MeO-DALT requires only a decent milligram scale and some empty gel capsules. For those of us who donÂ’t have the patience to grow anything or do our own extraction

Most contributors to this forum will be far more knowledgeable than I regarding both cluster headaches and those substances which have not as yet become mainstream weapons for the medical community in the fight against this uniquely painful affliction.
I told my 25-year-old son Michael's story on Clusterbusters sister site, Clusterheadaches. I repeat it here in case it could be of benefit to any sufferers out there looking for others with similar symptoms and histories who might not luck onto, as I did, the sister site. Bonkers is the screen name used on that site as well.
Michael got hit out of the blue one day while taking a shower. It knocked him to the ground, hurt like hell and scared the shit out of him. He was 12 years old. It wasn't the last one. We thought they were migraines since both my mother and I had those from our teens until we outgrew them at around 30. Our family doctor tried a variety of headache medicines on Michael over the next couple of years without success. He referred us to a Neurologist who, after conducting the usual tests, diagnosed the headaches as Clusters. He tried most, if not all, of the standard cluster remedies. Imitrex seemed to work best, but it too lost effectiveness over time. It got to the point where 2 shots did nothing. Michael got the most relief, and continues to get the most relief, from narcotics. Most of the time they deaden the pain enough that he doesn't black out. He's currently taking Methadone. Unlike the other narcotics, it hasn't required continually increasing dosages to be effective. The neuro continued experimenting with the various cluster remedies, including narcotics, until Michael was about 20 years old.
Then, he got into a particularly nasty car accident. He was life-flighted to the hospital with head injuries. Nothing too serious, but his headaches increased exponentially in both frequency and severity. He was uncontrollable during the spikes. Luckily, the pain usually increased to the point where he would black out. By now he was in the emergency room every couple of days where they've got some bigger guns. Nothing helped. They had to put him in a straight-jacket and eventually, with consent from the neuro, a drug-induced coma. They kept him there for a week. When Michael emerged from the coma he seemed better for awhile. But then they returned. Frustrated, the neuro decided Michael should take all the medicines at once! I don't think we even said goodbye. Degenerative disc disease and 5 fused vertebrae in my neck had me seeing a neck and back pain specialist who I trusted and considered a very good doctor. First off, I want to say that it's a life-saver that this community exists. This is my first post, and I figured I'd begin by sharing my busting story.
At the age of 29, I have been a cluster headache sufferer for 7 years. My attacks are yearly, at the end of the summer, generally lasting around two months. September and October are my cluster months. I am currently in a cluster phase.
Last year was my first year of being able to acquire the necessary medicine to attempt cluster busting. My approach may have been somewhat unorthodox, but the results were miraculous.
In 2012, my first (and only) cluster headache came late in the evening. I ate a modest dose of mushrooms at the onset of the attack. This was enough to receive a mild hallucinogenic effect, so I would estimate that it was around a gram and a half consumed. Eating these mushrooms did not abort that individual headache. I recall laying in bed in severe pain for several hours until the headache finally passed and I was able to sleep.
The next day, I began taking a small dose of LSD early in the day. Since LSD and Psilocin both have a tendency to prevent sleep, and since cluster headaches seem to be tied with the hypothalamus and circadian rhythms, I figured that my best bet would be to take the treatment early in the day so that I would be ready for a reasonably early bedtime. I figured that regulating my sleep patterns during this time may play a key role in ending the attacks.
I proceeded to take these low doses of LSD every day for five days. During that time, I had only a few shadows. They honestly didn't even feel much like cluster headaches, or migraines even for that matter, because they weren't unilateral. They felt a bit dull like a sinus headache. (I don't know about anyone else, but this is usually how I feel right before a cluster headache is coming. They build up to full pain much more quickly than a migraine. I go from having a dull pain across the front of my head to a full on excruciating eye pain within about five to ten minutes.)
In 2012, the headaches never came back. I did not have a single cluster headache for the rest of the year. I felt that I had more or less found an easy and painless method of aborting episodes.
I still have complete confidence that treating with tryptamines is the best and most effective method for treating this condition. I have had great success in 2013 when compared to my previous years, although there have been some rebound headaches this year. I'm hoping that I can refine my approach for future years.
In 2013, I did not use any mushrooms in my initial week of treatment. That is the biggest difference in the way I treated. The first headache also came late in the evening, when I was almost ready for bed. I did not feel prepared for a "trip" or anything that would keep me from a decent bedtime, so I went ahead and went to bed with that headache.

Hi,
My boyfriend Andy recently did his first bust with RC seeds and because it did something pretty dramatic (in a good way!), I wanted to share what he did and how it went. IÂ’m not sure how many people would be interested in his cycle pattern, so I have included that but at the bottom of this post.
His current cycle (can we still call it that?) began July 2010, and to varying degrees, is still with him now 10 months later. He has been keen to try busting, and luckily has no apprehensions about accidentally going on a long trip. He also takes no meds and only uses 02 to abort, so that has helped too.
But the info on these boards is that busting is most likely to work if you do it following a pain-free period and at the very first hint of a cycle starting up. If you try it when it full-blown cycle, it is least likely to work. So we waited. And waited. And waited. By last week, we figured this waiting game is pointless and what harm can it do to give this a go? So I ordered some shroom spores and 200 RC seeds from www.allsavia.co.uk - a very efficient service by the way: it arrived the next morning.
The aim:
To pick an initial dose and method, see if Andy felt anything, and use that for a starting point to see if heÂ’d be happy to take them again and whether more or less might work better next time. At this point, we still didnÂ’t think theyÂ’d do anything based on taking them at a time when he was getting big hits, and the fact that a deliberately low dose was chosen.
Choosing an amount to dose with:
the F.A.Q sheet suggests starting with 4-8 seeds. Most people seem to be busting with 50-60 seeds on average, so he decided on 20.
How he took the seeds:
Although itÂ’s recommended to crush your seeds into Â‘a fine powderÂ’, lacking a coffee grinder or pestle and mortar, he bashed his as much as possible and I would describe the result as a bit powder-like, but with a considerable amount of only half-battered, crunchy bits of seeds in the mix! This was placed in a glass with about a shotÂ’s worth of tap water added and maybe about a tablespoon of lemon juice (bottled kind) added. Put this in the fridge for just over an hour then he drank the whole lot, seed sludge included, put some more water into the glass, swilled it around and drank that too so that nothing was left.
What happened next
: He said he had a bit of a pain in the other side of his head to usual for about an hour, but that it was not a cluster headache. He also felt relaxed and very mildly stoned. What happened after that kind of stunned both of us. He went from 1 to 3 big hits every 24 hours (it had been that strong since New YearÂ’s Day) to being totally pain free for the first 3 days. Then he had a headache that needed 02 to abort, but it was much shorter and much less painful than what he was getting before.
Conclusion:
Well, overall, IÂ’d call this a pretty big success! In total, he had 3 of these lesser headaches over 7 days. By the 7th day, he very much had the restlessness back

I was researching the whole LSA to LSH thing a while back and never really found any definitive answers.Â  There are intelligent people that swear this conversion should not happen, there are intelligent people saying that it should.Â  (One thing I have never heard people claim is that Psilocybin is getting changed into LSH, pretty sure that is impossible)
One thing that I found quoted a number of times is that Owsley claimed that LSH is indistinguishable from LSD but after researching a bit I found that the LSH that Oswley was talking about was a different LSH than we are talking about here.
Also, I have never heard (and am really skeptical)Â  that the LSA is binding to acetaldehyde and changing to LSH in your body.Â  If it did, there would be no qualitative difference in any sort of peppermint conversion (assuming that actually works)---instead you would always just be getting an LSH trip.
In the mycoptopia link that was posted one guy writes what I think is happening.Â  Â
"When the seed is picked, it starts to decompose to yield the sedating LSA, which further decomposes to yield BUNK.
However once the seeds are decomposed enough, but prior to becoming bunk, their magic can be "revived" by the combination of an aldehyde (acetaldehyde, commonly; found in rum, sherry or fresh peppermint leaf) forming an adduct with the decomposed chemicals, thus re-forming the potent, stimulating LSH... or (whispers in the wind tell of a stable aldehyde in cinnamon essential oil) an even more potent adduct when mixed into the good, old, sacred-seed CWE."
The Cinnamon thing that people are talking about goes like this-instead of using acetaldehyde you use a type of aldehyde (there are a number of them) that is in cinnamon (cinnamaldehyde) that makes a somewhat different conversion with somewhat different results.Â  (all theory)Â  Here's some more info on that:
https://mycotopia.net/topic/70425-lsa-cinnamaldehyde-cinnamon-essential-oil/
The thing I would stress is that no one really knows what is being changed into what (if anything is being changed) and we won't know until we get good lab tests done on seeds before and after attempting this conversion.
What I can say for sure is that my experiences seem to indicate that SOMETHING is happening.Â  I have had seeds that knocked me down and sedated me more than almost anything else I have taken in my life and I have had seeds that reminded me of a light dose of LSD.Â  These experiences are so different that I know something is going on.Â  Only once have I had seeds that acted like LSD where I attempted no conversion but I washed it down with an alcoholic beverage which I later found out is high in acetdelhyde.Â
Native peoples have always claimed these seeds are visionary substances and to me LSA's sedating stupor does not fit that idea very well.Â  I suspect

Hey all. I’m new user of the forum and of the Clusterbusters site, although I’m fairly certain in the past I read through the countless resources available here. Anyhow I guess I’ll get on with my story and questions.
My story is likely similar to many others. I’m a male in my early 40’s and have been suffering from episodic cluster headaches for around ten or so years that I can remember. My episodes always occur in the Spring, always around the Spring time change, and typically every other year. My episodes begin with a single evening attack that develops over time into multiple attacks over a 24 hour period. They have a range of 3-5 months and usually consist of 8 insanely painful attacks with an ever present shadow pain that goes on even past my episodic end.  Unfortunately last year was an off year for me and my return to the nightmare started on March 21 (Cluster Headache Awareness Day). Yay me. I seriously dread life and dread speaking about this, as I know where time is going to take me and it infuriates and saddens me immensely.
In the early years prior to my diagnosis I had all but lost my will to live, having lived with a constant indescribable pain for which doctors offered zero assistance and medicines offered no relief.  I had visited countless doctors and neurologists who conducted countless tests and scans. It wasn’t until my second bout with episodes that I was diagnosed with CH, which was largely based on my own research. The neurologist prescribed several medications that failed to work. Hardcore pain meds, lithium, blood pressure meds, oxygen, oral triptans, etc. At that time I recalled reading that many ch sufferers were having luck aborting using triptan injections and I requested a prescription of my own. To my surprise the injection worked, although with their own odd side effects (made me feel as though I was being choked or short of breath and a slight burning discomfort). That first time was a miracle and I quickly fell asleep, having been living as an exhausted zombie for months, and slept for several hours for the first time in months. That first time held off attacks for nearly 24 hours before I again had to inject again. And again and again. My first script was for six injections so back to the doctor I went. As it turns out he wrote a script for more but insurance only would permit six injections a month! Are you kidding?! I have six to eight attacks a day and am being told I can only get six treatments a month? Anyhow I fought with the insurance company and they ultimately doubled what they would partially cover bringing me up to twelve single doses. I ended up only using the injection during the nighttime attacks, typically the attacks that wake you during sleep, and the massive attacks in the evening if I just couldn’t take it. I forced myself to endure the other daytime attacks and stockpiled everything I could on off months. This continued and I relied on the injections as my form of relief since then.

Hey everyone, I've been away for a while. My cycle petered out in February and I returned to my "baseline". Thanks again to everyone who supported me during the dark times.
My "baseline" isn't plain sailing by any means. For years I've been dealing with more or less daily chronic head/face pain. It ranges from a 1-4 most days, although maybe I'll get the odd day off here and there. This "baseline" pain is mainly on the left side of my head and tends to occur under my eye, in my upper teeth, in my ear, in my neck (with autonomic symptoms too). I will get more painful exacerbations (6-8) every so often (over endurance, in heat/humidity, when my sleep is disrupted) although those never quite seem to mirror the classical cluster attacks I get on the right side of my head - they can last 24 hours, maybe more, and always ramp up slowly rather than come out of nowhere.

Started getting right sided "shadows". I got back on the D3 regimen and upped my melatonin dosage. For a couple of weeks I didn't get any cluster attacks, and was able to get an occipital nerve block. I still haven't had any classical cluster attacks as of writing.
Over the course of these three weeks, my left sided pain has been worse. And it feels like my symptoms yo-yo from one side of my head to the other. On any given day, I might have pain switching sides multiple times. I can often notice it on both sides at the same time. Whilst I guess I should be grateful that I'm not experiencing full blown cluster attacks, I still find coping with almost constant pain very draining. I do wonder sometimes how I manage to get by, being a father to two young children, and holding down a senior, full-time job. text:
First of all, sorry for this not being just a "busting story", wanted to write an update since been away from the board (oops it's forums nowadays) for so long. And wanted to do this somewhere in the Login Required section.
I have not been sure what to call myself, since I was chronic prior to busting, received an incredible stretch of PF - around 3 years (wasn't even an episodic anymore - not according to my schedule) but when my little lover returned the form was more chronic than episodic. I have been busting regularly, plus doing the Vitamin D regimen. In the spring of this year something changed, it's hard to describe but the pain felt different. Not worse, stronger ... just different. 25 years everything has been familiar, the signs, the symptoms and the pain. Now it was something new. Then the form started to change more, I got more the kind of hits I could not "send back" (not abort, when I get shadows, I can react and "send CH back" to where it came from). Old tricks and treatments stopped working, one by one. Was not panicking yet but very uneasy and wondering. Then busting failed (MM, LSA), time after another and real cycle began with it's full force. This was routine since O2 worked just as well as always. Then in matter of days frequency of hits increased. At that point the alien feeling came into play. CH hits did not feel like CH hits. I have had them much, much worse in prescription medicated chronic years but somehow these weird new pain attacks freaked me out more than the volume of pain in the past. This was a long stretch and at the end of it I had developed something new for myself; I had periods of 12-24 hours getting hit every hour. Was not and had not been using
anything
to treat my CH at the end of this so I was puzzled. My first thought was that regular busting had changed or transformed the condition somehow and there just were no precedent patient cases of such to be found. I had LSD and 5-MeO-DALT available (haven't tried those) but I was so sure of this idea that hesitated to try those because of the possibility to make things worse. Well, now I have trashed that theory. Secondly, I though of some physical changes in the brain, because of the "alien" physical sensations and what I could find from other patients stories. At this point was rather desperate and went to the one place I thought I would never have to go to again; neurology department at the local hospital. My only wish was to get a head MRI taken. Didn't get one. Yesterday, I attempted a sublingual seed bust with Hawaiian Baby Woodrose seeds. My motivation for doing so was twofold:
1) I didn't want to go on a multi-day drug holiday from topiramate unless absolutely necessary, and thought it may be possible (ergot alkaloids and topiramate are metabolized by the same liver enzyme when ingested orally, so in theory I hoped to bypass that, allowing them each to work on their respective neuro receptors).
2) I reeeeeally didn't want to throw up
Preparation:
The seeds looked like dusty chocolate chips. I used a nail file to scrape off the outer part until they were shiny and light brown. My boyfriend, in solidarity (and because he had the day off
), decided to take some with me. For dosage approximations, I looked for "threshold" doses for women about my size, then added a few more for the potency reportedly lost in the sublingual ROA. I crushed the seeds with a hammer, threw them in my mouth, and held the whole mess there for 30 min. I chewed it occasionally, and was careful not to swallow any of the saliva. They taste like sunflower seeds and something herbaceous, not too terrible. I spit it all out after 30 min, rinsed my mouth, and waited. ***For anyone interested, I'm 5'6, about 115lbs, and I ended up crushing and taking 10 HBWR seeds SL for a threshold dose.
Experience:
Despite not having swallowed a bit, I was worried about nausea...especially because I was getting that familiar twinge behind my left eyebrow. I waited for a couple of hours, but the volcano behind my eye never erupted. I felt extremely restless, but didn't want to leave my boyfriend. At one point, he fell asleep. I started getting really hungry, so I went downstairs to make something for lunch.
That's when I realized that we have an *awesome* downstairs. I wandered around looking at all of our stuff...how do we walk by this antique pinball machine on that wall, every single day, and never even examine how intricately designed it is? I'm glad we have so many paintings with green in them, it's the kind of color you can get lost in because you have experienced so many different shades of green just by being outdoors. I looked outside and just completely lost interest in making lunch because it was so beautiful outside: the trees were offering up their leaves to be warmed by the sun, converting light into energy all while remaining perfectly still. We take our own surroundings for granted....
:-?
All of these felt like completely normal thoughts to be having, at the time. I just thought I was having a great day, with very minimal pain! I thought I was seeing the world like it really was, without my crushed-brain feeling. It was only later in the evening, after comparing notes with my boyfriend and after things returned to normal, that I gave in and admitted it was probably the seeds.
Conclusion:
I hope I am writing this too soon, and that I will be pleasantly surprised. I woke up this morning with the same twinge, radiating out into my temple.

Hello, everybody. This is my first post here. First off I like to thank everyone here for sharing their experiences and advice in this forum. It's great to hear what does and doesn't help, and feels good to know that there are so many others fighting the same fight as myself (although, now that I think about it, I probably shouldn't be glad that others are suffering... it just feels like I've got some sort of support group).
I've been getting CHs since 2001, usually about once a year, for 2-4 weeks, with 1-2 HAs a day. Each HA usually lasts about 2-3 hours. My last break was almost 2 years long, and I was sort of hoping I had somehow beat it, but then 2 weeks ago a new cycle started. This one's been different from my previous cycles, in that I'm having days where I get one HA after another, with maybe an hour break in between, and some lasting as long as 8 hours (had one that kept me awake from midnight til 8am). I pounded everything that was in the glass, and was actually pleasantly surprised that it didn't taste as bad as I was expecting based on things I've read on here.

ONLY use this for preparation of seeds for medical self treatment of cluster headaches!Â  I do NOT recommend anyone take anything they have not extensively researched and fully understand what to expect and accept the responsibilities that accompanies the action of self treatment.Â  Start with MINUTE doses to establish how you and your body react.

So a guy I know has experimented with extracting LSA from HBWR seeds.Â  Used an article he found on the internet and modified slightly.Â  Works perfectly.
Benefits:
1)Â  Dosing can be done
reliably
w/o the variability of LSA content from seed to seed
2)Â  A year or several years dosings can be prepared and kept in the freezer for as long as you need w/o any degradation in the LSA
3)Â  Stomach upset is significantly reduced, from prior experiences with HBWR.
4)Â  The vile 'seed sludge' is completely non existent
Need:
- 50 HBWR seeds (any quantity you choose to prepare)
- 2ea - 50ml bottle (liquer shot bottle)
- Crown Napatha (sold at hardware stores, for furniture restoration)Â  (washes some of the bad stuff out, and prepares the LSA to attach to the alcohol
- 50ml Drinking Alcohol 151 to 190 proof (the higher the better)
- 1 small plastic funnel (needs to fit inside the shot bottle)
- several coffee filters
Steps:
1) grind all your seeds into a powder (whatever method works best for you, um, my friend used a mortar and pestle
2) place the powder in the 50ml bottle (via funnel).Â  If you want to prepare more seeds, just use a larger bottle.
3) fill the bottle with the Napatha and shake whenever you think of it.
4) after an hour filter the seed powder from the Napatha.Â  Coffee filter in the funnel works good.Â  (the Napatha can be discarded now, as it's done it's job).Â  (or can just be put back in the jug for reuse another day).
5) spread the seed powder on the filter and lay out in a dark place to dry for about 2 hours.Â  You want the powder completely dry before moving past this step.Â  The Napatha evaporates completely without any residue.
6) In the second shot bottle using the funnel put the powder and top off with the drinking alcohol (151 or 190 proof).Â  Put in a dark place for two days to allow the LSA to be dissolved in the alchohol.
7) 2 days later - Filter as before with a coffee filter and funnel.Â  rinse any seed stuff that remains in the bottle, then pour the slightly brown alcohol back into the bottle.
8) top the bottle with additional alcohol to the 50ml mark.
Result:
Keep in the freezer (helps to tape the bottle with black electrical tape to prevent light from destroying the LSA.Â  You now have the result of 50 HBWR seeds in a solution of 50ml.Â  My dosings are with 7.5ml per dose = 7.5 HBWR seeds.Â  Each dose contains the same amount of LSA dose to dose.Â  I've found NO variability between dosings with the exact same ml dose.
Reduce stomach upset further:

I have been dreaming

Hello,I am new to the site. I am very thankful to have found people who understand the situation I find myself in. My first experience with a headache that only affected one half of my head (left side)happened about Fifteen years ago. I was at a work function, enjoying cocktails with friends when I suddenly hag a really bad headache on the left side of my head. I left the function and went back to my hotel room and took an Alka-Seltzer Plus cold medicine and it went away in about a half an hour. I didn’t have another episode until about four years ago. It started again with cocktails, and blew up very quickly into a terrible left side headache with what felt like a #2 pencil in my left eye. It felt like my sinuses were really blocked. I went t my doctor and was treated for a sinus infection with Amoxicillin. Everything went back to normal, until about three years ago, when I felt like I had another sinus infection, same headache. More antibiotics, problem solved. This happened about once a year until three months ago. I had a cocktail and the headache was back with a vengeance, I also discovered that Viagra was a trigger. I saw a nurse at a Minute Clinic and she prescribed saline spray, Flonase, and Prednisone. It didn’t help and the headaches came every night without alcohol or Viagra. I saw my doctor and she prescribed Amoxicillin which had worked for a sinus infection about six months earlier. Ten days later, I still had the headaches every night. I went back to my doctor and she prescribed Prednisone Z pack. That didn’t help, but she said she thought I was probably having Cluster headaches. She referred me to an ENT and a neurologist. The ENT looked at my sinuses and said that I have a deep sinus infection. He suggested minor surgery to open up the passages because my medical history indicates that I have sinusitis. He suggested that I may be experiencing cluster headaches, and while the surgery won’t prevent the headaches, it will likely help my sinus problem. The same day I met with the neurologist, I told him my story, and he said that I just described a textbook case of cluster headaches. He said that they are not curable and not easily treated. He looked at the two CT scans that I had from the last six months that showed the sinus infections and is still convinced that I have CH. I continue to have the headaches every night and sometimes during the day. The neurologist said the headaches could last from four to six weeks and then disappear for a year or more. About six weeks ago after a visit to the ER one night for headache pain, l had no headaches the next night. They were gone for two days and then came back. I thought I was out of the woods , but no luck. Anyway I am scheduled for minor sinus surgery in two weeks. There seems to be no rhyme or reason to these headaches. My doctor gave me a prescription for ten mg Prednisone tablets in case I feel a bad one coming. I had not taken any until last week, when I had a really bad headache.

I have been lurking here for quite some time, and have decided to tell my husbands story. About 20 years ago the clusters started, however as most of you know they were misdiagnosed. We finally found an excellent neurologist in 1996-ish, Dr, Nicholas in Cincinnati. He examined hubby, confirmed clusters and wrote a stack of scripts saying "start at the top and if that doesn't work move to the next. We have on idea what will work." Well we tried many of those drugs, triptans, topomax etc.. and they may have helped for a while but the side effects sucked. we were busy running our restaurant and he just seemed to work through them. Unfortunately on his own many times so I could rest to work the next day.
we eventually sold the restaurant and at our Neurologists suggestion to get out of Cincinnati, we moved to Sunny Florida. This has helped so much. We call my hubby solar powered. Whenever he feels bad, he sits and bakes in the sun. We have kept on a better schedule, gotten Vitamin D(sun) and the cycles were shorter and easier. Up until 2007. They started back with a vengeance. We did research on the alternative methods and felt it was he way to go after the bad time we had with the drugs. I would have posted this on the 11th, but I was in the hospital. So, I'm posting now.
My CH started on May 28th 2008. I was diagnosed pretty fast. An ENT who had saw me a few days before actually said my sinuses were clear, the best he'd ever seen, and he thought I had CH. I kinda blew it off, I'd read about them, and this sinus pain wasn't so bad. I think it was a shadow.
Chronic from the start, I'd have them every other day, around 12pm. Then eventually it moved to every day, and then to 3-8 times per day.
I tried most of the conventional drugs, and non-conventional, because I had so many allergies to things. I'm one of the lucky few that Narcotics work on, so my GP put me on Stadol. I had Radiofrequency Ablation to both occipital nerves and it made me wish I was dead.
I don't really feel like going through the details of this. So I'll skip ahead.
When I came here to clusterbusters, I was in hell. My doctors had pretty much crappity smacked me over and told me I had no options. When they told me this, I sobbed for a few days... and then I realized something.
If God/The creator/fate wanted me dead, there were a lot better ways to do it than to drive me to kill myself. No. I knew there was another option, the RC seeds, mushrooms, and LSD. I asked my Mom and Aunt if they knew how to get some, and they were shocked. "We're OLD! We don't hang around with people who do LSD anymore!"
I came to join Clusterbusters, and I was still in a bad place. If the first dose of seeds didn't change anything, I was going to put myself in the mental hospital. I know what suicide does to the people you leave behind. (A friend of my family's sister killed herself... and the things it did to them was horrible.)
The first use of the RC seeds, I felt tingling. Something was changing with my trigeminal nerve. The migraines I was having a few times a week stopped. Completely after the first dose.
I went from being in a place of "I'm not going to make it through this." to "I CAN survive it."
The CH got better and better with each dose, but I was still having to go to the ER pretty frequently when the hits got out of hand. Then it got to where I just could not get the seeds to go down.
An angel helped me get some Vitamin M, and for me, there are no words to say how thankful I am for that. It worked SO much better on me than the seeds.
In either June, or July of 2011, I finally made it into low cycle! I thought it was a myth, or at the very least, it didn't exist for me. Once I realized it, I was so happy.
Eventually I had to start growing my own.
I also eventually got to try LSD (which I'm also very thankful for. Just like the person who helped me with the Vitamin M, there are not enough words to convey my grattitude) and, from my experience, it works just as well for me as the mushrooms. 1/2 a tab has kept me in low cycle for almost 3 months. Mushrooms keep me in low about that long too.
Right now I'm having about 1-3 hits per day, pretty low level. I'm doing SO much better

So when I was 24 or something like that I learnt about busting. Being from Poland only Morning Glories were legal to posses but not to consume. First I tried 350 or 400 seeds. A rather strong effect, no hallucinations. Just air waving, brighter colors, perception heightened, I guess far from what mm or acid does to you or even HBWR.
I  eventually busted and dosed for 1.5 year longer every half year or so. I stopped after 1.5 years busting for control and didnt have to do it. I only got some shadows but they felt different, and usually went away when I pressed finger again temple and pressured occipital nerve at back of my head.
Oh yes control busting was usually 150 - 250 morning glory seeds.
Now I got a real amazing job and I got scared other day with as usual these shadows, ones that feel different to CH. so I got spooked, caring a lot for my work I busted 125 seeds but they are old. Only downside of Morning Glory is you get stomache issues and vasoconstriction all over yoyur body, you are very cold as your veins and artieries close up. your nose is cold, your limbs are cold, I assume its the black shell that I crush together with seed.
Anyway back to main story, I got scared and tried busting with 125 seeds, and 2 days later I woke up with familiar feeling of CH, that I forgotten these 4-5 years. It was maybe Kip 0,5 or 1 which is considered shadow, but when I woke up before I felt any discomfort in my eye socket I was very anxious, which is familiar to CH hits.
I am kinda worried I have started new cycle with busting, I havent busted for years and I got this feeling again only when I tried busting with 125 MG seeds, can I unbust myself or trigger CH with LSA?

That is why I will try my luck and perhaps there is a fellow CH sufferer who also busts and lives somewhere in Europe, I would be willing to share costs and be willing to travel to receive our 'medicine' which of cou
topic: My story as I am back after 4-5 years.

text:
Hi
I think i`m on my way to becoming a ECH for real. Before, busting didn't take away everything, like droopy and runny eye, shadows f ex. Like busting it may end an cycle for an ECH, it does the same for me now. Maybe not for a longer period of time as for an ECH, but it might be the start for it to work that way. But certainly long enough.
This time a year is usually high cycle a nasty one that would last and last, was so exited what was going to happen this time. Had two days with a very red and droopy runny eye, some nights waking up all the time. starting to get bitchy..... Got hit, then i dosed with HBWS. This was new to me. Had great success with RC seeds, so i was optimistic about it. Some way too much maybe, lol ;D I soaked only three seeds, drank the water only and got to bed. I actually thought i was going to sleep ;D Wich i did, until the effect woke me up. Funny thing to wake up from closed eyes visuals and lethargic body feeling. It felt like my feet's was ready for doing a marathon ;D After some hours my swellings was gone and i was "normal" again. Got some sleep, woke up and from there high cycle was just a bad thought. Amazing, three little seeds. I look at this tiny seeds in my fridge and really wonder, how can this be? There is a reason why these substances can be found out in nature
So i`m still PF and very excited what future will bring. I will try stretch my doses out with two days until i can maintenance maybe two times a year, but will stop with same amount of days in between every time activity increase. Very positive!!
In periods i take Magion+, that`s ionized Magnesium with spore minerals, that way i also get Lithium into my system. Also i take Taurin caps 1650 mg each two times a day, DHEA three times a day along with vit B2 and a cup of black coffee in the morning and one in the middle of the day. Periods coenzyme q10, as much as up to 900mg three times a day. This i start once my hands starts to get odd. When i got the res i wanted, i go down on dose then quit. Sometimes i take something called antisalt, that is Magnesuimsulfat and Kaliumsitrat, this has the same amount of Kalium and Mag as in a healthy cell, works well for me. exercise four to five times a week as well, that`s important part of it in my opinion.
Still keep my sleeping sched tight, diet is out of control :
That said, i have snack once in awhile, sometimes i eat food that would be triggers. A bit afraid to push it too far, so around two times a week max. But i eat MUCH MORE and gained match weight. Skin is better, same with my hair and my eyes look alive again.
What is so strange about this, being pf it`s like this terrible, horrible CH thing never happened!! I think that tells us how traumatic Ch are to a human. It`s like a distant memory, can`t recall the feeling of it. Just know it has been bad but it doesn't "stick" with me. All those years!!
Just wanted to share what i do. Everything is possible, only our minds sets the limits.
Your all so great,
